title: [AMDGPU] Detect trivially uniform arguments in InstCombine
summary: The developers generalized the optimization by extending `isTriviallyUniform()` to recognize not just constants but also SGPR-passed function arguments as uniform values. This single change enabled constant folding and simplification across multiple AMDGPU intrinsics (fcmp/icmp, readlane, class, exp2, log, rcp, sqrt, trig.preop) when they receive uniform inputs. The generalization strategy follows a pattern: identify a core property (uniformity) that enables optimization, then extend the property detection to cover more cases while maintaining correctness. This approach transforms a single-case optimization into a reusable transformation applicable to any intrinsic where uniform inputs guarantee deterministic outputs.
